graded_decisions.news_since: WWDC June 8 confirmed key catalyst; Morgan Stanley sees AI re-rating to $365-$385 on Siri/agentic demo; Apple Q2 rev $111.2B beat (Motley Fool / Yahoo Finance, 2026-06-01)
graded_decisions.comment: Timely dip buy ahead of WWDC. Caught the intraday low of the $306 session. WWDC AI positioning thesis intact; hold through June 8 event.

graded_decisions.news_since: Jensen Huang summoned to Senate Banking Committee testimony June 11 on China exports; Broadcom -15% miss June 5 triggered AI chip sector rotation; NVDA ex-div June 4 $0.25/share (Motley Fool / CNBC, 2026-06-05)
graded_decisions.comment: Well-timed trim near the short-term peak — NVDA fell 8.5% since the sell. However, 1 share trimmed vs 16-share starting position (6.25%) is too small; remaining 15 shares at avg $217 are now -3.2% and represent 34% of the long book.

graded_decisions.news_since: May payrolls +172K (2x consensus) June 5 spiked 10Y yield to 4.54%; Broadcom miss added Nasdaq pressure; Dow outperformed Nasdaq ~200bps on sector rotation (CNBC / HeyGoTrade, 2026-06-05)
graded_decisions.comment: Near-perfect exit — sold within $3 of the intraday high the session before a macro-driven tech selloff. De-risking the broad ETF leg was correct given yield spike risk.

graded_decisions.news_since: Same WWDC June 8 thesis; stock range-bound $308-$317 all week; no new positive catalysts between sessions
graded_decisions.comment: Second AAPL add (2 shares) executed on day-2 of the bounce, near the intraday high ($316.92 was the session high). Chased the June 2 recovery rather than waiting for a re-test. WWDC thesis is valid but entry $6.73 above the prior-day dip buy reduces margin of safety going into the event.

patterns: Conference-catalyst trades held with no trim discipline: MSFT surged 7.7% into Build 2026 (May 28-Jun 1, $427→$460) then reversed -8.4% by June 5 with no trim — textbook buy-the-rumor/sell-the-news; the 3-share MSFT position is now the portfolio's largest realized drag.

Same-name double-entry on consecutive sessions without a pullback: AAPL bought at $309.18 (Jun 2, strong entry on the dip) then 2x more at $315.91 (Jun 3, +2.2% higher, near intraday high). Scaling into a bounce rather than a pullback inflates average cost and cuts margin of safety.

Trim sizing too small relative to position: NVDA trim was 1/16 of the position (6.25%). Remaining 15 shares at avg $217 represent 34% of the long book — a minor trim did not meaningfully reduce concentration risk.
QQQ proceeds immediately redeployed into single-name risk: Sold QQQ (de-risking) and bought 2x AAPL in the same session, partially neutralizing the hedging benefit of the ETF exit.

Fully correlated tech basket: AAPL + MSFT + NVDA + QQQ all high-beta tech with near-1.0 intra-week correlation; when Broadcom missed and payrolls spiked yields, every name moved down together with no offset from non-tech exposure.
suggested_adjustments: Pre-event trim rule: Reduce any position by 30-50% within 1 trading session of a major product event (WWDC, Build, GTC, earnings) when the stock is >5% above its 20-day SMA. Captures conference premium before the sell-the-news reversal. Immediate application: AAPL before WWDC on June 8.

Scale-in only on pullbacks, not bounces: A second entry on the same thesis must be priced at least 1.5% below the prior entry. If the stock has bounced more than 2% since the first buy, wait for a re-test of that level before adding.

Single-name position cap at 20% of long book: If any name exceeds 20% of long market value, trim to 20% the next session regardless of conviction. NVDA at 34% is the immediate violator — trim ~7 shares at market open.

No same-session redeployment from ETF/hedge exits: When closing or trimming a broad ETF position, hold proceeds in cash for at least 1 full trading session before deploying into individual names. Forces a deliberate reallocation decision rather than an automatic rotation.
summary: Week June 1-5: portfolio -3.12% vs SPY -1.22%, VT -1.46%, SSO -2.39% (SSO tracked almost exactly 2×SPY, +0.06pp path drift — negligible). Underperformed all passive benchmarks by 1.7-1.9pp. The QQQ and NVDA trims were well-timed (both near short-term peaks) but MSFT's -8.4% post-Build 2026 reversal with no trim, plus a chased AAPL add at $316, more than erased those gains. NVDA 34% concentration and 100% tech-correlated book are the dominant structural risks. Immediate priorities: enforce pre-event trim on AAPL before WWDC June 8, and trim NVDA back to 20% weight.
